lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: So many guests invite as here are writ.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Sirrah, go hire me twenty cunning cooks.

lines.speaker: SECOND SERVINGMAN
lines.text: You shall have none ill, sir; for I’ll try if they can lick their fingers.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: How canst thou try them so?

lines.speaker: SECOND SERVINGMAN
lines.text: Marry, sir, ’tis an ill cook that cannot lick his own fingers:

lines.speaker: SECOND SERVINGMAN
lines.text: therefore he that cannot lick his fingers goes not with me.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Go, be gone.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: We shall be much unfurnish’d for this time.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: What, is my daughter gone to Friar Laurence?

lines.speaker: NURSE
lines.text: Ay, forsooth.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Well, he may chance to do some good on her:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: A peevish self-will’d harlotry it is.

lines.speaker: NURSE
lines.text: See where she comes from shrift with merry look.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: How now, my headstrong! where have you been gadding?

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: Where I have learn’d me to repent the sin

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: Of disobedient opposition

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: To you and your behests, and am enjoin’d

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: By holy Laurence to fall prostrate here,

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: And beg your pardon: pardon, I beseech you!

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: Henceforward I am ever ruled by you.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Send for the county; go tell him of this:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: I’ll have this knot knit up to-morrow morning.

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: I met the youthful lord at Laurence’ cell;

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: And gave him what becomed love I might

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: Not stepping o’er the bounds of modesty.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Why, I am glad on’t; this is well: stand up:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: This is as’t should be. Let me see the county;

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Ay, marry, go, I say, and fetch him hither.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Now, afore God! this reverend holy friar,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: All our whole city is much bound to him.

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: Nurse, will you go with me into my closet,

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: To help me sort such needful ornaments

lines.speaker: JULIET
lines.text: As you think fit to furnish me to-morrow?

lines.speaker: LADY CAPULET
lines.text: No, not till Thursday; there is time enough.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Go, nurse, go with her: we’ll to church to-morrow.

lines.speaker: LADY CAPULET
lines.text: We shall be short in our provision:

lines.speaker: LADY CAPULET
lines.text: ’Tis now near night.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Tush, I will stir about,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: And all things shall be well, I warrant thee, wife:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Go thou to Juliet, help to deck up her;

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: I’ll not to bed to-night; let me alone;

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: I’ll play the housewife for this once. What, ho!

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: They are all forth. Well, I will walk myself

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: To County Paris, to prepare him up

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Against to-morrow: my heart is wondrous light,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Since this same wayward girl is so reclaim’d.

stage_directions.text: Enter Capulet, Lady Capulet, Nurse, and two Servingmen.

glosses.definition: expert kitchen staff — for the wedding feast
proverbial test of a good cook — the cook who tastes his own work
wayward wench — not a literal charge

annotations.title: Juliet's strategic penitence
annotations.body: Juliet, newly returned from the Friar's with the potion, kneels to Capulet and asks forgiveness. Her performance is so convincing that Capulet, delighted, moves the wedding from Thursday to Wednesday — one day earlier. The irony is cruel: Juliet's theatrical obedience has accelerated the timetable she was trying to evade. The scene demonstrates, quietly, how consequential even small forms of performance become in the play; Juliet's growing competence as a self-concealing actor will carry her into the tomb in the next scene. Capulet's generous response here ('My heart is wondrous light') also reminds us that he is not a monster — just a patriarch whose affections operate through his dynasty.

trials.prompt: What unintended consequence follows from Juliet's apology?
options: Her father forgives her but the marriage is still Thursday
Capulet, delighted, moves the wedding from Thursday to Wednesday — one day earlier, tightening the Friar's timeline
